ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: tɩɩsɩŋ, yaŋ arɩ kʋŋkala sɩɩ nyʋ tinteeŋ lɛ fa tuo, bɛɛ wɩaa La TƖƖNA Wɩa fa ha bɩ leŋe duonuŋ nɩɩ. Nʋʋ ma kala fa tuo dɩ ʋ pɛrɛ tintee-la lɛ,
translated: Tilson, you must not take the initiative to speak to the elders, because La TINA is the one who will handle the matter. But if you speak to them again, it will be a problem.

ref: This includes the birds and the animals that came out of the boat.
text: a pɛ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩɩ wiesu lɛ: Zaaraa, bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ arɩ kɩalɩŋ kala sɩ to ŋ harɩŋ lɩɩ lɩɩŋ daboro-la tʋɔŋ.
translated: A piece from the Book of Matthew: Zechariah, Baruch, listen to me, the one called Jesus says to the people, look and see, the kingdom of heaven is near.

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb mɩɩra kɔ dɩa ʋ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk teeŋ Mamɩrɛ lɛ, a kpagɛ Kɩrɩaf Aba . Lee-la nɛ Abɩraham arɩ Aɩzɩk fa hɔŋ.
translated: This is where Jacob met Isaac in the land of Mamre, and he built an altar to the Lord. This is the place where Abraham and Isaac dwelled.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ama mɩ jaŋ bil niiŋ mɩ dɩŋ pɛgɛ. Kɛŋ ŋ haala, ŋ biiriŋ, arɩ ŋ bii-haalaa, dɩ ma kala jʋʋ lɩɩŋ daboro-la.
translated: "Ama is my name. I am going to the house. And he goes, he comes, and he is going, the child is playing with the ball."

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ beŋ aŋ bʋl, “Oo, ʋ nɛ. Gɩrɩŋ kʋŋ nɛ kɛnʋ chaŋ, kʋŋ nɛ sɛnɛ kɛŋ Josɛf pʋɔsɛ.”
translated: He said to his brother, "Ah, he is. The father said, 'My sons, you said Joseph's son.'"

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Ruuma fa ŋaa Nahɔ haala dɔŋɔ ʋ sɩ bile nɛ aŋ ha bɩ jaa ka ba lʋl biiriŋ deemba: Teba, Gaham, Tahas arɩ Maaka.
translated: Ruuma said to Nahɔ, "They have gone with us to the village of the elders: Teba, Gaham, Tahas, and Maaka."

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: Ʋ hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Nowa aŋ bʋl, “Wɩa fa ŋmɩɛsa hɛ tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ dɩ la tɩma suoli aŋ-na dii. Bie deeŋ nɛ jaŋ leŋ dɩ la tʋɔŋ pɩŋ dɩ-bala.”
translated: Nowa said, "He spoke the truth, and that one does not know the way of the ancestors. But they say loudly, 'Listen to the elders.'"

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Lee-la kala ŋ sɩ na, mɩ jaŋ joŋo pɩŋ, ŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa a kaa mʋ wuu.
translated: Listen, I am saying your name is good, I know you are the one who loves me.

ref: When the brother with the red thread was born, they named him Zerah.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ naambile ma ba fa sɩ joŋ ŋmɛŋ-fɩaŋ vɔɔ nɩsɩŋ ma kʋ lɩɩ. Ba hɛ ʋ yɩrɩŋ Zɛra.
translated: Zɛra is the village where the children and people went with the man. They are from Zɛra.

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Wɩa bɩra bʋla pɩ Adam ma, “Ŋ sɩ jegile nɩɩ ŋ haala niiŋ a dii tɩɩnɛŋ-la mɩ sɩ sɩ ŋ sɩ́ dii, mɩ kalɩba hɛ tinteeŋ lɛ nɛ a tɩŋ ŋ wɩaa. Ŋ mɩɩsɩŋ kala lɛ ŋ jaŋ tɩma suoli aŋ-na dii.
translated: God said to Adam, "You will certainly die on the day you eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil; you will become like God, knowing good and evil. The ground is cursed because of you; by the sweat of your face you will eat bread."

ref: As Joseph's brothers sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were loaded with all kinds of spices that they were taking to Egypt.
text: Ba sɩ hɔnɔ dɩ ba dii kɩaa, ba na dɩ Ɩsɩmɛl doho nɩaa kɛŋ ba bʋntaŋŋaba kɩɛla to dɔŋɔ a lɩɩ Gɩlɩad tinteeŋ dɩɩrɩŋ a kʋkɔ. Ba bʋntaŋŋaba fa chʋŋ tegisiŋ nɛ, nʋʋ-sʋɔrʋsʋŋ, tɩɩ-kel-zɔŋŋɔɔ arɩ tɩɩ-nɛŋ-zɔŋŋɔɔ ba sɩ yie joŋo hɛ kʋdiilee lɛ ba sɩŋ a kaa mʋmʋ Ijipiti tinteeŋ.
translated: Translation here

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Rakɛl bʋl, “Mɩ dɩ mɩ malɩma Liya lobo nɛ woruŋ, ama mɩ lobo diiu nɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ hɛ bile yɩrɩŋ Nafɩtalɩ.
translated: Rachel said, "I am with Leah, the daughter of Laban, but you have taken me as a foreigner." Rachel is the wife of Naphtali.

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: Magɩdɩɛl arɩ Ɩram. Ɛdɔm tinteeŋ kuoroo arɩ ba dɩ-hɔnɔɔ nɛ ŋɩɩ. Ɛsɔ nɛ ŋaa Ɛdɔm tɩŋŋaa kala nyɩmma.
translated: "Magidie is Rama. Edward goes with his people in the village. He is Edward the hunter."

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Abɩmɛlɛk yɩrɛ Abɩraham a pɩɛsʋ, “Bɛɛ nɛ tɩɩ ŋ ŋaa wɩɩŋ deeŋ la lɛ? Bɛɛ wɩɩ nɛ mɩ cheyiŋ ŋ lɛrɛ wʋbɔŋ-balɩŋ deeŋ kʋ hɛ mɩ dɩ mɩ nɩaa lɛ? Ŋ ŋaa wɩɩ fa sɩ bɩ maga dɩ ŋ ŋaa nɛ mɩ lɛ.”
translated: Abimelech said to Abraham, "Where do you come from? Where is your home? Where is your wife and your daughter, your servants, your livestock, and your property? Your wife has taken my daughter and your property."

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: Ɩsɩmɛl ma wɩaa, mɩ nɩa nɛ, mɩ jaŋ pɛ ʋ lɛ a leŋ dɩ ʋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ woruŋ. Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ʋ ŋaa kuori-gentisiŋ fii arɩ balɩa nyɩmma, dɩ ʋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ woruŋ.
translated: Isimel my brother, he is not here, he went to the place where the doctor is located. He went to the place where the pharmacist and nurse are located, the place where the doctor is located.

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Wɩa bʋla pɩ Nowa arɩ ʋ biiriŋ,
translated: someone

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ sʋl Wɩa a bʋl, “Ou, mɩ TƖƖNA, mɩ kʋhɩaŋ Abɩraham Wɩa, pɛ mɩ lɛ dɩ mɩ na wɩɩ-la mɩ sɩɩ chɛ jɩnɩŋ, a dagɛ ŋ zile pɩ mɩ kʋhɩaŋ.
translated: I am the one called Wɩa, the one who is called. O Father, my ancestor Abraham the father, and I have made my name known to them, my covenant is with them, and we have established our covenant with my ancestors.

ref: Not a bird, animal, reptile, or human was left alive anywhere on earth.
text: Kʋŋ na-kala sɩ kɛŋ mɩɩsɩŋ a hɛ dʋnɩa lɛ sʋʋ: Bagɩlaa, pʋsʋŋ, diibiisiŋ arɩ fillee arɩ nuhuobiine kala.
translated: The language of the people of the country of the world: Bagila, Pusung, Diibeesi, and others.

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Aɩzɩk sii nɩmɛ a bɩra mʋ Bɛɛsɩba tinteeŋ.
translated: Aizik goes to Nimba, he/she goes to Besiba in the evening.

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: Mɩ jaŋ leŋ dɩ ŋ doho nɩaa pʋŋ, a ŋaa tintee-bal. Mɩ jaŋ pɛ ŋ lɛ, a leŋ dɩ ŋ yɩrɩŋ yɩrɛ, a to ŋ lɛ a pɛ nɩaa lɛ.
translated: "Mijaŋ is the one who is the leader, and his tintee-bal. Mijaŋ is the one who goes to Yirin Yire, and comes to the one who is."

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ ŋaanɩŋ naasɩŋ nɛ ŋɩɩ. La TƖƖNA Wɩa fa sɩ ŋaa wɩa-nyuŋ arɩ tinteeŋ,
translated: The Great Chief Wia has given the name to the one who is the leader of the people of the land.

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ bʋla pʋ, “Mʋ na ŋ naambiiriŋ arɩ pʋsʋ-la, dɩ wɩɩ nɛ jʋʋba, ŋ mɩɩra kʋ bʋla pɩmɛ.” Ŋɩɩ nɛ ʋ nyɩmma tɩmʋ ʋ lɩɩ Hɛbʋrɔŋ fuonaa-la lɛ a mʋ. Josɛf sɩ mʋ yi Sɛkɛm,
translated: And it happened that he said to them, "My son is the one who has done this, but you are lying; he will punish you if you do not bring him." And they did not want to listen to the Hebrew language and his people. Joseph went to Shechem.

ref: but he refused and said, “My master isn't worried about anything in his house, because he has placed me in charge of everything he owns.
text: Ama Josɛf vɩa aŋ bʋl, “Mɩ kʋhɩaŋ joŋ ʋ dɩa arɩ kʋŋkala a hɛ mɩ nɩsɩŋ lɛ nɛ mɩ bibeŋ.
translated: Mother Joseph said, "I listen to you, you see the truth, and I believe and live by it."

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Ŋɩɩ nɛ Jekɔb sʋl Wɩa a bʋl, “Mɩ nyɩmma Abɩraham Wɩa, mɩ nyɩmma Aɩzɩk Wɩa, ŋ nɛ bʋla pɩmɛ, dɩ mɩ mɩɩra mʋ mɩ tinteeŋ mɩ nɩaa teeŋ, dɩ ŋ jaŋ pɛ mɩ lɛ dɩ mɩ nɩsɩŋ jʋʋ woruŋ.
translated: And Jacob spoke to his sons, saying, "I am with Abraham your father, I am with Isaac your father, but you should not be divided into factions, for if you divide yourselves, you will be scattered, and I will not be with you anymore."

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Tapʋl torimuŋ chɛɛŋ Abɩraham beŋ sɩpaaŋ a mʋ na lee-la, dɩ ʋ hɛ dɩ-bolii.
translated: The Lord said to Abraham, "My son, listen carefully, and you will become a great nation."
